title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in Norway
description: Norway's gender-neutral marriage law came into force in 2009, allowing same-sex couples to marry and to adopt jointly.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Coquille Indian Tribe recognizes its first same-sex marriage
description: Kitzen and Jeni Branting married in 2009 in the Coquille tribe's plankhouse at Coos Bay, Oregon, becoming the first same-sex couple whose marriage the tribe recognized; Kitzen was a tribal member.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mexico City passes a same-sex marriage law
description: Mexico City's legislative assembly approved same-sex marriage with joint adoption rights in 2009, the first jurisdiction in Latin America to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: New Hampshire, Maine and Washington, D.C. pass same-sex marriage laws
description: In 2009 the US state of New Hampshire (with step adoption only), the state of Maine and the District of Columbia each passed same-sex marriage legislation; Maine's law was repealed by referendum before taking effect.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Hungary's registered partnership law takes effect
description: Hungary passed and brought into force a registered partnership law for same-sex couples in 2009, without joint adoption rights.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Colombia expands partnership rights for same-sex couples
description: In 2009 Colombia extended the legal rights available to same-sex partners, building on earlier recognition, though joint adoption remained excluded.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Nevada and Washington expand domestic partnership rights
description: The US states of Nevada and Washington passed and enacted laws in 2009 expanding the rights of registered same-sex partners.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Austria passes a registered partnership law
description: Austria's parliament approved a registered partnership law for same-sex couples in 2009, excluding joint adoption; it entered into force the following year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Colorado and Wisconsin enact limited partnership laws
description: The US states of Colorado and Wisconsin passed and brought into force laws in 2009 granting same-sex couples a limited set of partnership rights.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Finland allows step adoption for same-sex couples
description: Finland legalized step adoption in 2009, letting a person adopt the biological child of their registered same-sex partner.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Adoption by same-sex couples becomes legal in Scotland
description: In 2009 same-sex couples in Scotland gained the legal right to adopt children.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Maine voters repeal the state's same-sex marriage law
description: A 2009 referendum in the US state of Maine overturned the same-sex marriage law passed earlier that year, blocking it from taking effect.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Serbia adopts anti-discrimination legislation
description: Serbia enacted anti-discrimination legislation in 2009 that covers sexual orientation as a protected ground.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Delaware bans private-sector discrimination based on sexual orientation
description: The US state of Delaware added sexual orientation to its anti-discrimination law covering the private sector in 2009.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: United States enacts the Matthew Shepard hate-crimes law
description: The Matthew Shepard Act became US federal law in 2009, extending hate-crime protections to offenses motivated by sexual orientation and gender identity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Alberta adds sexual orientation to its human rights legislation
description: In 2009 the Canadian province of Alberta wrote sexual orientation into its human rights act, the last jurisdiction in Canada to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Argentina ends its ban on gay people in the military
description: Argentina lifted its ban on openly gay people serving in the armed forces in 2009.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: The Philippines ends its ban on gay people in the military
description: The Philippines lifted its ban on openly gay people serving in the armed forces in 2009.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Uruguay ends its ban on gay people in the military
description: Uruguay lifted its ban on openly gay people serving in the armed forces in 2009.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: International Transgender Day of Visibility is established
description: Michigan-based activist Rachel Crandall Crocker created the observance in 2009 to celebrate living transgender people, complementing the mourning focus of the Transgender Day of Remembrance.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Iceland elects the world's first openly gay head of government
description: Jóhanna Sigurðardóttir took office as prime minister of Iceland in 2009, the first openly gay person anywhere to lead a national government.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Annise Parker is elected mayor of Houston
description: Annise Parker won the Houston mayoral election in 2009, making it the largest city in the United States led by an openly gay mayor.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Diego Sanchez becomes the first openly transgender Capitol Hill staffer
description: Hired in 2009 as a legislative assistant to Representative Barney Frank, Diego Sanchez was the first openly transgender person to work on Capitol Hill; in 2008 he had been the first on the Democratic National Committee's Platform Committee.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Barbra Siperstein joins the Democratic National Committee
description: Barbra "Babs" Siperstein was nominated and confirmed as an at-large member of the Democratic National Committee in 2009, its first openly transgender member.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Israel recognizes adoption rights of a same-sex male couple
description: Uzi Even and his life partner became the first male couple in Israel whose right to adopt was legally acknowledged, in 2009.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Rugby player Gareth Thomas comes out
description: Welsh rugby star Gareth Thomas came out in 2009, the first known top-level professional male athlete in a team sport to do so while still competing.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Carol Ann Duffy is named Poet Laureate of the United Kingdom
description: Carol Ann Duffy's 2009 appointment made her the first openly lesbian or gay person to hold the post of UK Poet Laureate.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Amy Andre takes charge of San Francisco Pride
description: In October 2009 activist Amy Andre was appointed executive director of the San Francisco Pride Celebration Committee, the organization's first openly bisexual woman of color in that role.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Rabbi Ron Yosef comes out on Israeli television
description: In 2009 Ron Yosef became the first Israeli orthodox rabbi to come out, doing so on the investigative program Uvda in an episode about conversion therapy in Israel; he kept his position as a pulpit rabbi.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Siddur Sha'ar Zahav prayer book is published
description: The first complete Jewish prayer book written to address the lives of LGBTQ as well as straight Jews appeared in 2009, produced by San Francisco's Congregation Sha'ar Zahav.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Portugal legalizes same-sex marriage
description: Portugal's same-sex marriage law passed and came into force in 2010, without joint adoption rights.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Iceland legalizes same-sex marriage
description: Iceland's same-sex marriage law passed and came into force in 2010, including joint adoption rights.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Argentina legalizes same-sex marriage
description: Argentina's same-sex marriage law, including adoption rights, passed and came into force in 2010, making it the first country in Latin America to open marriage to same-sex couples.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in Mexico City
description: Mexico City's law allowing same-sex couples to marry and adopt jointly came into force in 2010.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in New Hampshire and Washington, D.C.
description: The same-sex marriage laws of the US state of New Hampshire (with step adoption only) and the District of Columbia came into force in 2010.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mexican Supreme Court upholds nationwide validity of Mexico City marriages
description: In 2010 Mexico's Supreme Court ruled that same-sex marriages contracted in Mexico City are valid throughout the country, although other jurisdictions are not required to perform them.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tasmania recognizes same-sex marriages from other jurisdictions
description: In 2010 the Australian state of Tasmania began recognizing same-sex marriages performed elsewhere.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Federal judge strikes down California's Proposition 8
description: In 2010 US District Judge Vaughn Walker ruled that California's Proposition 8, which barred same-sex marriage, violated the Fourteenth Amendment's equal protection and due process guarantees.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Austria's registered partnership law takes effect
description: Austria's registered partnership law for same-sex couples came into force in 2010, without adoption or IVF access rights.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ireland passes a civil partnership law
description: Ireland's parliament approved civil partnership legislation for same-sex couples in 2010, without adoption rights.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: New South Wales enacts a limited partnership scheme
description: The Australian state of New South Wales passed and brought into force a limited partnership law in 2010; joint adoption rights followed in September of that year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: New South Wales opens adoption to same-sex couples
description: The Australian state of New South Wales passed legislation in 2010 permitting same-sex couples to adopt children.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Denmark allows adoption by same-sex couples
description: Denmark passed legislation in 2010 permitting same-sex couples to adopt children.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Arkansas and Florida adoption bans are lifted
description: In 2010 the US states of Arkansas and Florida ended their bans on adoption by same-sex couples.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Serbia ends its ban on gay people in the military
description: Serbia lifted its ban on openly gay people serving in the armed forces in 2010.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Australia ends its ban on transgender military service
description: Australia lifted its ban on transgender people serving in the armed forces in 2010.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Fiji decriminalizes homosexuality
description: Fiji removed criminal penalties for consensual same-sex activity in 2010.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First legal pride parade in Russia is held in St. Petersburg
description: In 2010 St. Petersburg hosted the first gay pride parade in Russia to take place with legal permission.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Guinness World Records recognizes Thomas Beatie's childbirth record
description: In 2010 Guinness World Records listed transgender man Thomas Beatie as the world's first married man to give birth.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Amanda Simpson becomes first openly transgender US presidential appointee
description: Amanda Simpson was appointed senior technical adviser in the Commerce Department's Bureau of Industry and Security in 2010, the first openly transgender presidential appointee in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Kye Allums plays NCAA basketball as an openly transgender athlete
description: In 2010 Kye Allums, a transgender man on George Washington University's women's team, became the first openly transgender athlete in NCAA basketball.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Phyllis Frye becomes the first openly transgender judge in the United States
description: Phyllis Frye was appointed to the bench in 2010, the first openly transgender person to serve as a judge in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mary Albing becomes the ELCA's first openly lesbian minister
description: Mary Albing was ordained by the Evangelical Lutheran Church in America in 2010, its first openly lesbian minister, serving a congregation on the south side of Minneapolis.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Chai Feldblum joins the US Equal Employment Opportunity Commission
description: Openly lesbian lawyer Chai Feldblum became the first openly LGBT person to serve on the EEOC in 2010.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Donna Ryu is appointed to the federal bench in Northern California
description: Donna Ryu's 2010 appointment as a judge of the US District Court for the Northern District of California made her that court's first Asian-American woman, first Korean-American and first lesbian judge.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tyler McCormick wins International Mr. Leather
description: In 2010 Tyler McCormick became the first openly transgender man, the first wheelchair user, and the first person from New Mexico to take the International Mr. Leather title.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: New York legalizes same-sex marriage
description: The US state of New York passed a same-sex marriage law in 2011, and it came into force the same year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Suquamish Tribe legalizes same-sex marriage
description: The Suquamish Tribe adopted marriage equality in 2011, with the change taking effect that year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ireland's civil partnership law takes effect
description: Ireland's civil partnership legislation for same-sex couples came into force in 2011, without adoption rights.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Isle of Man enacts civil partnerships
description: The Isle of Man passed and brought into force a civil partnership law in 2011, including joint adoption rights.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Illinois and Rhode Island enact civil union laws
description: In 2011 the US states of Illinois (with joint adoption rights) and Rhode Island passed civil union laws that took effect the same year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Liechtenstein enacts registered partnerships
description: Liechtenstein's registered partnership law for same-sex couples was approved and came into force in 2011.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Delaware and Hawaii pass civil union laws
description: The US states of Delaware and Hawaii approved civil union legislation in 2011; Hawaii's law took effect in January 2012 and Delaware's in 2013.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: United States ends the ban on openly gay, lesbian and bisexual military service
description: With the 2011 repeal of the Don't Ask, Don't Tell policy, openly gay, lesbian and bisexual people gained the right to serve in the US armed forces.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tony Briffa is elected mayor of Hobsons Bay
description: Tony Briffa's election in November 2011 in the City of Hobsons Bay, in the suburbs of Melbourne, Australia, is believed to have made them the world's first intersex mayor.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Elio Di Rupo becomes Prime Minister of Belgium
description: Elio Di Rupo took office on 6 December 2011 as the first openly gay man to head a national government.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Chaz Bono competes on Dancing with the Stars
description: Chaz Bono appeared on the 13th US season of Dancing with the Stars in 2011, the first time an openly transgender man starred on a major network television show for something unrelated to being transgender.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Harmony Santana earns a major acting award nomination
description: Harmony Santana became the first openly transgender actress to receive a major acting award nomination, named by the Independent Spirit Awards in 2011 as Best Supporting Actress for Gun Hill Road.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
